texto: You wake up one morning and all your spiritual feelings are gone. You pray, but nothing happens. You rebuke the devil, but it doesn't change anything. You go through spiritual exercises...you have your friends pray for you...you confess every sin you can imagine, then go around asking forgiveness of everyone you know. You fats...still nothing. You begin to wonder how long this spiritual gloom might last. Days? Weeks? Months? Will it ever end? ....it feels as if your prayers simply bounce off the ceiling. In utter depression, you cry out, "What's the matter with me?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Floyd McClung: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a fe. O trecho “You wake up one morning and all your spiritual feelings are gone. You pray, but nothing happens. …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, depression, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e mudanca.
